title: NASUN STORY
heading: Ceremony
paragraph1: The other day at the beach I watched my kids draw in the sand with their fingers. They were drawing their favorite Pokemon characters, and I got reminded of the old adage: to know where you are going, you have to know where you came from. It started with this I thought to myself, it started with them drawing on the dirt ground with sticks; to drawing hunting stories on cave walls; to gathering around the evening fire to hear about the great famine by their shaman; to epic poets orating tales of gods and heroes in the King’s court; to the lyrical poet sharing their personal blues on the road for a meal; to the power of the theater bringing the community together for a cathartic climax; to the imaginative freedom of the modern novel; to the audiovisual spectacle of cinema; and here we are today, the world sharing memes over the Internet for an emoji reaction and a lonely chuckle. So now what? Where do we go from here?
paragraph2: Everything we need is everywhere around us today, amazing technology, affordable tools and an overflow of talented people. We know we could be building some mind-bending stuff but every day the possibility seems more distant. It's like that dream you just had but can’t remember, already fading into the ether.
paragraph3: We can make this leap into a cool future timeline we know is out there, but really, we have no idea in which direction to jump. Copy and paste won’t do it. Neither will typing the perfect prompt. There is no Youtube tutorial for this. Posting the perfect meme to get a dozen plus laughing Carlos emojis, or posting your daily gms to get a hundred gms back wont cut it. While scientists and researchers are using technology available today to unlock the mysteries of the universe, this is what content creators are flailing around with. So we all know we have everything we need today to come together and create wonderful, beautiful, terrifying things, but no one knows where to start.
paragraph4: When Oedipus learns the truth, he takes Jocasta’s gold pins and stabs his eyes out. It is at this shocking moment, the climax of the tragedy, that the audience in the outdoor amphitheater witnesses the veil of illusion torn away, and they stare with Oedipus into the abyss. They gasp at the horrifying truth, not only the fate of the great King, but theirs as well. All our constructed facades of reality, culture, society, laws, relationships, are violently stripped away and become meaningless when confronted with not only the chaos and suffering of existence but the Gods mocking us. And it is this moment that Oedipus’ disfigured tragedy is transformed into something beautiful.
paragraph5: The theater, this constructed art, forces us to face this existential crisis together in this communal setting and makes us realize that while the tragedy of life is our fate, we do not face it alone. This structured spectacle transports the community through story, in dramatic form joining the epic and lyrical, the Apollonian plastic arts and the Dionysian orgiastic chaos, to weave this experience for the audience to empathize with Oedipus and feel this terrible truth together at the same moment. A powerful moment of catharsis, and even though it’s just a moment it is enough for the differences between each audience member to disappear, overtaken by a fleeting communal experience where they witness together the tragic nature of existence defined by suffering, so that the sharing itself becomes beautiful. As Nietzsche exalted in ‘The Birth of Tragedy,’ the audience becomes cleansed, healed together as a community knowing we are not alone. This is the power of art. A shared perspective where we feel we are not alone. And that is a beautiful thing to feel in this cold, indifferent universe.
paragraph6: I remember when I was a teen staying up all night with a novel, because I couldn’t put it down, getting the shivers reading the last words as dawn broke. Or going to a Saturday matinee on a hot summer day to sit in the cold dark theater and walking out two hours later, squinting in the late afternoon sun with just having had my mind blown. Or going through all the vhs tapes of the sections by countries and directors in Kim’s Videos on Bleecker Street, feverishly traveling through the history of film. Or going to the 2nd floor Big Apple Comics on 89th Street and Broadway to rifle through the crates and talk about the latest issues from John Byrne, Walter Simonson and Frank Miller with the Upper Westside comic book community, a diverse group of people from all backgrounds, races and ages. And as I go through these wonderful memories what strikes me as the common thread is the power of stories. Many of my favorite memories revolve around storytelling in some form or another. This need to hear them, to share them, to experience them. To hear your close friend tell you that funny, embarrassing shit that happened at school the other day. Your aunt telling you something about your great grandfather from another era, a family story, offering a clue to who you might be or become. You sit there being fully transported to another place and time, feeling those emotions, and sometimes it's so amazing you cry tears of joy.
paragraph7: What does the future of storytelling hold for us now?
paragraph8: Nasun believes we are at a crossroads. We have the chance right now to do something truly special. We have the means today to go on the greatest adventure ever known to man, to embark on a journey so wondrous to new places unimaginable before. To have experiences that will shake us to the core of our being, experiences beyond comprehension, experiences that will flip our perception of reality, forcing us to face our past and future selves, experiences so profound, so sensational, so deep and overpowering that we will have no choice but to destroy our old value systems and embrace the new ones borne from the myths we are about to create. Because for these experiences to exist, we will need to create them together. And this will be our privilege, joy, and a shared burden for all of us to go on this journey together.
paragraph9: Nasun believes that we are at a crossroads. That humanity is about to embark on its greatest story ever. That we now have the tools, the means, to fully unleash our imaginations to create the new myths in new formats that will give us unforgettable experiences. We will together as a global community not only share these powerful experiences but we will make them together. And this profound journey will transform our understanding of what is possible.
paragraph10: Whatever this need is, this drive, this obsession and passion, this pervasive, incessant, undying need to create or communicate or share.  Stories can be the most powerful means of bringing people together, of transforming perceptions and showing us that we are not alone, that this life is shared.
paragraph11: And the other night as I watched my kid draw Eevee on their tablet, I remembered her drawing on the beach, and I was again reminded that it started with drawing on sand and we are still drawing on sand today.
paragraph12: So much has changed but maybe not much at all. It all passes in a blink of an eye so lets make the most of it.

call.title: Join Our Story
call.description: Be part of crafting the next evolution of human narrative.

points: Build bridges between digital and physical worlds
Forge new myths for the modern age
